Scenario.title: Water Treatment Plant Cyber Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Intermediate A sophisticated cyber attack targeting water treatment plants, leveraging spear phishing and drive-by compromise techniques to gain initial access, followed by exploitation of vulnerabilities and privilege escalation to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Spear Phishing via Compromised Email Accounts
stage.description: The attackers send targeted emails to water treatment plant employees, posing as legitimate communication from a trusted source, tricking them into revealing sensitive information or clicking on malicious links.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers embed malicious code in a website visited by water treatment plant employees on their personal devices, compromising their systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers exploit an API vulnerability in the SCADA system software used by the water treatment plant to execute malicious code.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers establish a web shell on the compromised system to maintain access and execute further attacks.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers use elevated privileges to move laterally within the network and gain control of critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers utilize anti-forensic tools to erase logs and cover tracks, making it difficult for investigators to detect the attack.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt the operation of the water treatment plant by overwhelming the system with traffic or flooding it with requests.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated cyber attack targeting water treatment plants, leveraging spear phishing and drive-by compromise techniques to gain initial access, followed by exploitation of vulnerabilities and privilege escalation to disrupt operations.
